sourceScope.sourceReference: New York State Department of Financial Services final adoption of the Third Amendment to 11 NYCRR 103 (Insurance Regulation 213)
sourceScope.sourceStatusNote: The file is the final adoption text for the Third Amendment, not a companion note or FAQ.
sourceScope.rawSourceRoot: C:\Dev\NAIC Valuation Manual Course\NAIC Valuation Regulation

observedSectionWindows.note: Third Amendment title, footnote revision, and certification.

boundaries: Keep the final adoption text separate from the core Regulation 213 wave.
Keep the Amendment No. 1 FAQ addendum and the other amendment-history PDFs in the same folder out of this wave unless a later amendment-history plan explicitly opens them.
Preserve page locators and line references where available, but expect the text layer to remain page-image-backed for exact wording.
Keep the two-page window narrow so the boundary logic stays easy to review.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No Regulation 213 core-wave extraction in this batch.
No FAQ addendum pages in this wave.
No other amendment-history PDFs in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Third Amendment text, footnote revision, and certification
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the amendment title, footnote revision, and certification together and stop at the end of page 2.

proposedBatches.title: Regulation 213 Third Amendment final adoption text and certification

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the amendment title, the footnote revision, and the certification as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the amendment title, the footnote revision, and the certification as one narrow review-only slice.

reviewStandards.finalAdoption: Treat the source as final adopted regulatory text and not as a companion note or FAQ.
reviewStandards.stateRegulation: Treat the source as New York regulatory material tied to Regulation 213.
reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat direct references to regulatory duties as mapped regulatory context, but do not promote the amendment itself into a separate approved export.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat incorporated-manual and certification language as documentation context if it appears.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Valuation Manual and the amended footnote as mapped context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title and introductory text as background unless the text imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat dense or ambiguous amendment wording as requiring human review unless the page image confirms the wording.

validationRequirements: The plan JSON must exist and parse cleanly.
The plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
The dedicated batch-definition file must exist and stay synchronized with the plan.
The batch must stay review-only by default.
The source must remain final adoption text and not be promoted.
The source must stay separate from the core Regulation 213 wave and the FAQ addendum.
The source should remain page-image-backed for exact wording where needed.
